title: Animations
objectives: Drive animations with controllers, tweens and curves

sections.markdown: Animations interpolate values over time to create motion. Flutter offers implicit animations (AnimatedFoo widgets) for simple cases and explicit animations (AnimationController) for full control.
Forgetting `with SingleTickerProviderStateMixin` (the vsync) or not disposing the controller — leaks and errors. Also, animating layout-affecting properties on many widgets can cause jank.
Prefer transform/opacity animations (cheap, GPU-friendly) over animating layout. Wrap animated subtrees in RepaintBoundary, and use AnimatedBuilder to rebuild only the animating part.

flashcards.back: Implicit: set a target on an AnimatedFoo widget. Explicit: drive an AnimationController yourself for full control.
A vsync (TickerProvider, e.g. SingleTickerProviderStateMixin) and must be disposed.
Transform and opacity (GPU-friendly); avoid animating layout for many widgets.

quiz.explain: You set a target and Flutter animates implicitly.
vsync ties the controller to the frame signal.
Transform/opacity are GPU-cheap; layout animations can jank.

challenges.text: Build a fade+slide intro with AnimationController + Tween.
Implement a custom Hero-like transition and keep it at 60fps.

interviewQuestions: What does AnimationController need and why dispose it?
How do you keep animations at 60fps?
AnimatedBuilder vs rebuilding the whole widget?
